methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $97M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Nomagic, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Kacper Nowicki, is evaluated against a comparables universe of nine private firms and one public company (277810.KQ). The peer set includes five material robotics platforms: 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M).

Two valuation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method uses Nomagic's $97M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $184M. The peer-median method across six same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a $1.1B implied valuation, though the peer set exhibits substantial dispersion ($300M to $10.0B), reflecting heterogeneity in capital efficiency and market positioning within the robotics sector.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $157M (low), $630M (median), and $1.2B (high). The median estimate of $630M sits between the stage-progression floor and peer-median estimate, reflecting tension between Nomagic's funding history and peer valuations. Key uncertainties include the degree to which outliers like 1X Technologies ($10.0B) and Fairmat ($350M) represent comparable go-to-market approaches, and whether Nomagic's differentiation supports valuation toward the peer median or closer to the stage-progression estimate.
